{{Infobox character
| name = Karoshan queen (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)
| titles = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Queen]]}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, First of her name, Queen of Damasca}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Queen]]}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Damasca]]}}
| appears_in = Traveler's Gate
| introduced = [[The Crimson Vault (novel)|The Crimson Vault]]
}}

''See also: [[Karoshan queen (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy) Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The title of [[Queen of Damasca]] has been held by several significant figures. The first queen was a legendary figure who unified the nation of [[Damasca]], defeated an [[Elysian Incarnation]] in combat, and established a bloodline connection to the territory of Ragnarus by breaking its seal. Another notable ruler was [[Cynara]], who possessed red eyes and a [[ruby]] smile. [[Leah]] currently serves as the [[Queen of Damasca]]; she is a [[Ragnarus Traveler]] who feels a duty to protect [[Cana]] from the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]]. Although she holds this royal position, her authority is complicated by the fact that her father is still alive and remains the King. [[Leah]]'s leadership involves directing [[Travelers]] to oppose the will of their respective [[Territory]] [[Incarnations]].}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] commissioned the palace in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cynara]] the First, also known as the original [[Ragnarus Incarnation]], once ruled as the [[Queen of Damasca]]. During her reign, she established an ancient pact concerning the entry requirements for the [[Crimson Vault]]. Driven by rage over the manipulations of the [[Elysian Incarnation]], she launched a red wooden javelin toward [[Helgard]]. Later, a second [[Queen]] [[Cynara]] successfully sealed the [[Avernus Incarnation]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] is the original [[Ragnarus Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] made an ancient pact regarding who can enter the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The second [[Queen]] [[Cynara]] sealed the [[Avernus Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] the First was enraged at the [[Elysian Incarnation]]'s manipulation.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] is also known as the First of her name.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cynara]] the First was the [[Queen of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen]] [[Cynara]] launched a red wooden javelin at [[Helgard]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“We can handle this, Leah,” Queen Cynara said, launching a red wooden javelin at Helgard.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
